description:
## What's Wrong
Install script fetches `releases/latest` unauthenticated and downloads a tarball without any integrity verification (checksum/signature). It may also fail due to GitHub API rate limits.

## Where
- scripts/install.sh: uses https://api.github.com/.../releases/latest and grep/sed parsing
- scripts/install.sh: downloads release asset and untars with no checksum

## Why It Matters
- Security: users execute a network script piping to bash; we should verify what we download.
- Reliability: rate limiting or JSON format changes can break installs.

## Suggested Fix
- Publish checksums (e.g., SHA256SUMS) as release assets and verify before install.
- Prefer GitHub release page redirect for latest tag, or allow `BD_VERSION` override.
- Use `jq` when available; fallback to robust parsing.

## Acceptance
- Installer verifies integrity of downloaded binary.
- Installer supports explicit version pinning.
title: Installer hardening: checksum verification + rate-limit robustness

description:
**Problem**
Phase 7 implementation must be verified to complete the realtime v0.5 epic.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §18: admin.status correctness under load
- Depends on: Phase 7 implementation + Phase 7 test infrastructure

**Design**
Deterministic tests for:
- Subscribe streaming: events arrive in order, no gaps
- require_min_seen: subscription blocks until watermark condition met
- admin.status consistency: watermarks + segment stats are coherent under load
- admin.status monotonicity: watermarks never regress
- Concurrent subscriptions: multiple clients receive same events

**Acceptance**
- [ ] tests/phase7_subscribe.rs: streaming subscription tests
- [ ] tests/phase7_admin_status.rs: admin.status under load tests
- [ ] Test: watermarks are monotonic during concurrent mutations
- [ ] Test: segment stats match actual WAL files
- [ ] cargo test phase7_ passes

**Files:** tests/phase7_subscribe.rs (new), tests/phase7_admin_status.rs (new)
title: Phase 7 tests: streaming + admin.status under load

description:
**Problem**
DecodeError is rich (non_canonical, decode limits, trailing bytes), but replay collapses decode failures into wal_corrupt and there is no visible mapping for replication error frames. REALTIME_ERRORS.md expects actionable codes (invalid_request, frame_too_large, batch_too_large, non_canonical).

**Where**
- src/core/event.rs: DecodeError
- src/daemon/wal/replay.rs: decode errors -> wal_corrupt
- replication ingress path (Phase 5)

**Design**
- Add DecodeError -> ErrorPayload mapping for replication/IPC ingress.
- Use non_canonical for canonical CBOR violations; size-related errors map to frame_too_large/batch_too_large; other semantic decode errors -> invalid_request.

**Acceptance**
- Replication ERROR frames include realtime codes for decode limits/non_canonical.
- Non-canonical CBOR is not reported as wal_corrupt in network paths.

**Files**
- src/core/event.rs
- src/daemon/* replication ingress code (bd-3m5.17/48)
- src/daemon/ipc.rs (if IPC shares mapping)
title: Replication ingress: map DecodeError to realtime codes

description:
**Problem**
Git sync and checkpoint code (~9,700 lines) lives inside the beads-rs monolith. It's independent infrastructure the daemon calls INTO, but being in beads-rs means agents can accidentally couple it to daemon internals, and it can't be consumed independently.

**Design**
Create \`crates/beads-git/\` and move the entire git module there.

Move map:
- \`beads-rs/src/git/mod.rs\` → \`beads-git/src/lib.rs\`
- \`beads-rs/src/git/error.rs\` → \`beads-git/src/error.rs\`
- \`beads-rs/src/git/sync.rs\` → \`beads-git/src/sync.rs\`
- \`beads-rs/src/git/wire.rs\` → \`beads-git/src/wire.rs\`
- \`beads-rs/src/git/observe.rs\` → \`beads-git/src/observe.rs\`
- \`beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/**\` → \`beads-git/src/checkpoint/**\` (all 10 files)

Cargo.toml deps:
\`\`\`toml
[dependencies]
beads-core = { path = "../beads-core" }
git2 = { version = "0.20", features = ["vendored-libgit2", "vendored-openssl"] }
sha2 = "0.10"
hex = "0.4"
flate2 = "1"
tar = "0.4"
serde = { workspace = true }
serde_json = { workspace = true }
thiserror = { workspace = true }
tracing = { workspace = true }
bytes = { workspace = true }
tempfile = "3"
\`\`\`

Compat shim in beads-rs during transition: \`pub use beads_git::*;\`

**Acceptance**
- [ ] \`crates/beads-git/\` exists with Cargo.toml and all modules
- [ ] Added to workspace members in root Cargo.toml
- [ ] beads-git has zero imports from daemon, surface, api, cli, or beads-rs
- [ ] beads-rs depends on beads-git, compat shim preserves \`crate::git::*\` paths
- [ ] \`docs/CRATE_DAG.md\` updated: \`beads-git -> beads-core\` allowed
- [ ] DAG lint updated to include beads-git
- [ ] \`cargo check --workspace\` passes
- [ ] \`cargo test --workspace\` passes
- [ ] \`just dylint\` passes

**Files:** \`crates/beads-git/\` (new), \`crates/beads-rs/src/git/\` (becomes shim), \`Cargo.toml\`, \`crates/beads-rs/Cargo.toml\`, \`docs/CRATE_DAG.md\`, \`lints/beads_lints/tests/crate_dag_policy.rs\`
title: Extract beads-git crate from beads-rs/src/git/

description: tests/integration/daemon/subscribe.rs uses manual polling + 30s MAX_WAIT. Use ReadConsistency require_min_seen + wait_timeout_ms tied to expected seqs (from receipts) so tests block deterministically without repeated sleep/reconnect loops.
title: Subscribe integration tests: replace sleep loops with read gating

description:
**Problem**
Equivocation paths are opt-in; it is easy to forget to exercise them in model runs.

**Design**
- Add a documented command (and optionally a CI/just target) that runs the repl model with --equivocate.
- Keep default equivocation off to avoid inflating baseline runs.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] There is a named command/target that runs with --equivocate.
- [ ] No change to default model behavior.

**Files**
- justfile or scripts/ (if adding a target)
- beads_stateright_models/examples/repl_core_machine.rs (docs/help text if needed)
title: Stateright repl: add equivocation coverage run

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] TxnId derivation incorporates actor identity (or full `Stamp`).
- [ ] New test proves distinct actors produce different TxnIds for identical WriteStamp values.
- [ ] Call sites updated accordingly.
description:
**Problem**
`txn_id_for_stamp` (mutation engine) derives `TxnId` from `store_id` + `WriteStamp` (wall_ms, counter) only. The actor/replica identity is not included, so two actors with the same wall_ms/counter can produce identical TxnIds. This breaks the invariant that TxnIds are unique across actors and makes idempotency/debugging brittle.

**Files**
- src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs
design:
**Design**
- Change `txn_id_for_stamp` to include actor identity (e.g., accept a full `Stamp` or `ActorId` and use it in the UUID v5 namespace/name).
- Ensure the generated TxnId is stable for the same actor+stamp but distinct across actors.
- Add a test that two different actors with the same wall_ms/counter produce different TxnIds.
title: TxnId generation should include actor identity

description:
**Problem**
Some CLI command handlers use direct `println\!()` instead of going through render.rs, causing:
- Inconsistent output formatting
- Untestable output (no way to capture/verify)
- Mixed patterns: some commands use `render::render_*()`, others use `print_ok()`, others raw println

Examples found:
- `src/cli/commands/close.rs:19`: `println\!("✓ Closed {}: {}", id.as_str(), reason_str);`
- `src/cli/commands/update.rs:122`: direct println for success message
- Similar patterns in reopen.rs, delete.rs, claim.rs, unclaim.rs

**Design**
1. Audit all command handlers in `src/cli/commands/` for direct println usage
2. For each, either:
   a. Add a `render_<command>()` function to render.rs, OR
   b. Use existing `print_ok()` wrapper if output is simple "✓ Done" style
3. Ensure JSON mode (`ctx.json`) is handled consistently — render functions should check this

Example refactor for close.rs:
```rust
// Before (close.rs:19)
println\!("✓ Closed {}: {}", id.as_str(), reason_str);

// After (render.rs)
pub fn render_close(id: &BeadId, reason: Option<&str>) {
    let reason_str = reason.map(|r| format\!(" ({})", r)).unwrap_or_default();
    println\!("✓ Closed {}{}", id.as_str(), reason_str);
}

// close.rs
render::render_close(&id, args.reason.as_deref());
```

**Acceptance**
- [ ] No direct println\!() in src/cli/commands/*.rs (except for --json output via print_json)
- [ ] All human-readable output goes through render.rs functions
- [ ] render.rs has consistent pattern: render_<command>() for each command with custom output
- [ ] cargo clippy passes
- [ ] cargo test passes

**Files:** src/cli/commands/close.rs, src/cli/commands/update.rs, src/cli/commands/reopen.rs, src/cli/commands/delete.rs, src/cli/commands/claim.rs, src/cli/commands/unclaim.rs, src/cli/render.rs
title: Move direct println\!() calls from CLI handlers to render.rs
